regions.us-ports:
**US PORTS WEATHER BRIEFING | July 9, 2026**

Today in Miami, a Heat Advisory is in effect with dangerous heat index values posing a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions and port operations, with southeast winds at 7 knots, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability supporting embarkation and disembarkation activities. Port Everglades and Port Canaveral will experience similar conditions with southeast winds at 7-9 knots and clear skies, creating favorable conditions for vessel movement and tender operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Miami will transition to partly cloudy skies on Day 1 with east-southeast winds increasing to 9 knots and less than 10% rain probability, followed by Day 2 conditions featuring east-southeast winds at 11 knots and rain showers with a 16% rain probability that may affect some shore excursions. By Day 3, southeast winds will increase to 14 knots with drizzle conditions and less than 10% rain probability returning to the Miami area and surrounding ports. The strengthening southeast wind trend over the three-day period will gradually increase working conditions for tender operations, though no significant marine advisories are expected through the forecast window.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today elevated heat is a priority for Nassau and Freeport and elevated winds are expected Day 2 at 20kt for Grand Cayman and Ocho Rios, with current conditions in Nassau E 5kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability. Embarkation and shore excursions in Nassau and Bimini will operate under east winds E 10kt and overcast skies today with less than 10% rain probability, so crew should stage outdoor activities with overcast conditions in mind. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Day 2 brings ESE 20kt, overcast skies, and 15% rain probability, producing elevated wind conditions that could require modified tendering and dock procedures in Grand Cayman and Ocho Rios. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 trends show ESE 18kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, indicating sustained moderate ESE winds that will influence maneuvering and small-boat operations for La Romana and Samaná.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today a Heat Advisory is in effect for San Juan with heat index values reaching 100 F; current conditions are easterly winds E 12kt with drizzle and 26% rain probability, and the advisory elevates heat stress for passengers during outdoor shore excursions and extended embarkation or disembarkation periods. For operations today San Juan is forecast E 16kt with drizzle and 26% rain probability, so tendering and gangway teams should account for steady east winds during boarding and secure loose deck gear. Over the next 24 to 48 hours San Juan is expected E 16kt, overcast, and less than 10% rain probability, and St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. Maarten should plan for overcast deck conditions during scheduled shore calls. Beyond 48 hours San Juan is forecast E 14kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, and St. Kitts and Antigua should monitor the forecast for potential thunderstorm-driven temporary suspensions of open-deck activities and adjustments to docking schedules.
regions.western-caribbean: Today: Priority alert for elevated heat with heat index reaching 102F today and 101F on day two, and a significant thunderstorm threat by day three with 51% rain probability; ports most affected include Cozumel and Belize City, current Cozumel conditions NNE 4kt, partly cloudy, 26% rain probability. For port operations and shore excursions today, Cozumel and Costa Maya should account for ENE 9kt with thunderstorms and 26% rain probability while elevated heat index values (102F) increase heat stress for outdoor activities and gangway staffing. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Costa Maya and Roatan will see E 11kt with thunderstorms and 26% rain probability, accompanied by the elevated heat index near 101F that could affect prolonged outdoor excursions and crew rotations. Beyond 48 hours the forecast shifts to E 10kt with thunderstorms and a 51% rain probability that may affect shore excursions and tendering in Cozumel, Roatan, and Belize City, so embarkation teams should prepare for thunderstorm-related delays.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today elevated easterly winds are the priority alert: E 23kt is forecast for Aruba, Curaçao and Bonaire today, with Day 2 also at E 23kt and Day 3 at E 20kt. Today Aruba reports E 18kt with drizzle and 13% rain probability, and the sustained E 18kt winds will require extra caution during gangway operations and tendering for Aruba and Cartagena. Over the next 24 to 48 hours the outlook shows Day 1 E 23kt with drizzle and 13% rain probability and Day 2 E 23kt with drizzle and 20% rain probability, sustaining elevated wind handling for shore excursions and embarkation procedures at Curaçao and Bonaire. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 is forecast E 20kt with thunderstorms and 26% rain probability, and the continued E 20kt easterlies will keep small-boat procedures and deck operations under restricted protocols for Aruba and Cartagena.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today Significant Weather Day 3: rain showers, 67% rain probability are expected to affect port operations in Barbados, St. Lucia and Martinique with forecast winds E 14kt. Today current conditions at Barbados are E 13kt, overcast, 22% rain probability; embarkation and tendering at Bridgetown should account for steady E 13kt winds and overcast skies with 22% rain probability. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 1 is forecast ENE 15kt, drizzle, 22% rain probability and Day 2 ENE 16kt, drizzle, 11% rain probability, with sustained ENE winds likely to influence dock maneuvers and shore-landing operations at Antigua, St. Kitts and St. Maarten. Beyond 48 hours the forecast returns to E 14kt with rain showers and 67% rain probability, and that 67% rain probability is expected to impact shore excursions and berth operations at Barbados, St. Lucia and Grenada.
regions.los-angeles: Today the port complex is seeing SSW winds at 4 kt with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting routine embarkation and disembarkation operations at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach. Over the next 24 hours Day 1 forecasts S winds at 8 kt with foggy skies and less than 10% rain probability, which will reduce visibility for visibility-sensitive shore excursions and tendering at Catalina Island and Marina del Rey. Between 24 and 48 hours Day 2 shows SSE winds at 10 kt with foggy skies and less than 10% rain probability, indicating continued reduced visibility for morning embarkation windows at Catalina Island, Long Beach, and Los Angeles / San Pedro. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 is forecast S winds at 9 kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, showing a trend toward persistent low cloud cover that may limit aerial sightseeing and sun-dependent shore activities at Catalina Island and Marina del Rey.
regions.ensenada: Today Ensenada harbor is reporting SSW 2kt winds with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability; embarkation and disembarkation at Ensenada and Punta Banda are proceeding under standard procedures, though overcast light levels may lengthen visual gangway checks. For the next 24 to 48 hours Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos are forecast WSW 7kt and foggy with less than 10% rain probability, and foggy conditions could reduce morning visibility for shore excursions and tender operations. Beyond 48 hours the forecast shifts to WSW 8kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos, with slightly higher WSW winds near 8kt supporting routine deck and embarkation activities. Operations planning should incorporate the current SSW 2kt reading, the WSW 7kt foggy forecasts for the next 48 hours, and the WSW 8kt overcast trend with less than 10% rain probability when scheduling tender runs and shore-excursion start times for Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today Elevated Heat Day 3: Heat index reaching 102F will most affect Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz, while current conditions are WNW 7kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability. Today embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo will operate under WNW 7kt winds and clear skies with less than 10% rain probability, requiring standard ship-to-shore coordination for gangway and tendering. Over the next 24-48 hours the outlook shows Day 1 with WSW 8kt winds, partly cloudy skies, and less than 10% rain probability followed by Day 2 with WSW 7kt winds, drizzle, and 12% rain probability, with La Paz and San Jose del Cabo experiencing the WSW wind shift and light drizzle on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 returns W 10kt winds with clear skies and 10% rain probability while elevated heat with heat index reaching 102F should be incorporated into shore excursion timing and crew hydration plans for Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz.
regions.mazatlan: Today Elevated Heat Day 2 (Heat Index reaching 103F) and Elevated Heat Day 3 (Heat Index reaching 101F) are highlighted for Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata while thunderstorms with a 43% rain probability are present under overcast skies and an east wind of 3 kt, which may affect embarkation and outdoor shore excursions at Mazatlan and Altata. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 2 forecasts northwest winds 7 kt with overcast skies and 19% rain probability at Mazatlan and Topolobampo, and Elevated Heat Day 2 (Heat Index reaching 103F) will require shortened outdoor exposure during shore excursions at Topolobampo and Altata. By Day 3 northwest winds increase to 10 kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, while Elevated Heat Day 3 (Heat Index reaching 101F) should be factored into all outdoor cruise operations and passenger planning. The short-range trend shows a wind shift from southeast 8 kt on the Day 1 outlook to northwest 7–10 kt on Days 2–3 and a decline in rain probability from 43% to less than 10% across all ports, supporting more stable embarkation windows but maintaining elevated heat indices in operational plans.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today elevated heat is forecast for Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita with heat index reaching 102F; elevated heat is expected on Day 2 in Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita with heat index reaching 103F; and excessive heat is forecast on Day 3 for Yelapa and Chacala with heat index reaching 105F; current conditions are NNE 2kt with drizzle and a 38% rain probability that may affect outdoor shore excursions. Thunderstorms are forecast today with ENE 7kt and a 38% rain probability and may affect tendering and waterfront excursions at Yelapa and Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita are forecast SSE 7kt with drizzle and a 19% rain probability and elevated heat reaching a heat index of 103F. Beyond 48 hours conditions trend drier with SW 8kt and partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability while excessive heat with a heat index up to 105F is forecast for Yelapa and Chacala.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today SSW 1kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability, supporting routine embarkation and disembarkation operations at Barcelona and scheduled shore excursions in Valencia. Day 1: S 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability; southerly S 9kt conditions are forecast during morning berthing at Palma de Mallorca and will modestly influence tender angles for Ibiza operations. Day 2: E 8kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability; easterly E 8kt during midday operations will be the prevailing condition for Valencia and Malaga and should not inhibit scheduled shore transfers. Day 3: ESE 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability; the trend toward ESE 9kt with persistent overcast on Day 3 indicates continued light easterly flow affecting Cadiz and Gibraltar for late departures and routine maneuvering.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today S 1kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability, so very light southerly winds and partly sunny skies support routine embarkation, disembarkation and shore-excursion loading at Naples and nearby Civitavecchia. Over the next 24 hours (Day 1) SW 7kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability will be present across Marseille, Nice, Monaco, Genoa and Livorno, with SW 7 kt flow and overcast skies permitting scheduled port-side operations and tendering. During the 24–48 hour window (Day 2) SW 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability is forecast for La Spezia, Livorno, Civitavecchia and Naples, where light SW 9 kt winds and persistent overcast should allow normal maneuvering and gangway procedures. Beyond 48 hours (Day 3) SSW 8kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability extends to Sardinia, Corsica, Split, Dubrovnik and Venice, indicating continued light southerly winds and overcast conditions that support planned cruise navigation and shore operations.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today NE 7kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability at Athens (Piraeus) with minimal wind and cloud cover expected to allow normal embarkation/disembarkation at Piraeus and routine shore excursions to Santorini and Mykonos. Over the next 24 hours Day 1 is NE 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, a profile that will favor tendering operations in Santorini and gangway operations in Mykonos and Piraeus under overcast skies. Between 24 and 48 hours Day 2 shows a shift to SE 7kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, a wind change that will be relevant for approach and berth planning at Rhodes, Cyprus (Larnaca/Limassol) and Izmir. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 remains SE 7kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability, indicating continued light SE winds that support scheduled calls and routine shore excursion planning at Haifa, Alexandria and Istanbul.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today the forecast flags Day 2 rain showers (ESE 5kt, 73% rain probability) expected to affect Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway, with an elevated Day 3 drizzle chance (ESE 4kt, 52% rain probability) that may affect the same ports. Today Juneau reports ESE 5kt, partly cloudy, 13% rain probability; embarkation, tendering, and scheduled shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway will operate under these conditions. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 2 is forecast ESE 5kt, rain showers, 73% rain probability and is expected to affect port operations and outdoor excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows ESE 4kt, drizzle, 52% rain probability and that lighter precipitation may affect timing of outdoor activities and require flexible scheduling for Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway.
